words.mean_en: another word for 'yes'
a big fruit that is yellow inside and has a rough brown skin
able to take in and hold something,especially liquid: an absorbent cloth
Something that is porous has many small holes, so liquid or air can pass through, especially slowly
relating to ships, sailing, or sailors

1. to travel on the water: The ship sailed along the coast
2. to control a boat with sails: We sailed the yacht down the river
a person who sails ships or boats as their job or as a sport
words or ideas that have no meaning or that are not true: It's nonsense to say that Jackie is lazy

1. to let something fall: I dropped my watch and it broke
2. to fall: The glass dropped from her hands
3. to become lower or less: The temperature has dropped
4. (also drop somebody off) to stop your car and let somebody get out: Could you drop me at the station? - He dropped me off at the bus stop
5. to stop doing something: I'm going to drop geography (= stop studying it) at school next year
**drop in** to visit somebody who does not know that you are coming: We were in the area so we thought we'd drop in and see you
**drop off** to fall asleep: She dropped off in front of the TV
**drop out** to stop doing something with a group of people: I dropped out of the football team after I hurt my leg

1. one of the floors of a ship, plane or bus: He stood on the lower deck of the ship and looked out to sea
2. (American) (British pack) a set of 52 cards for playing games
to fall or drop heavily

to make sounds to show that you are happy or that you think something is funny: His jokes always make me laugh
**laugh at somebody or something** to laugh to show that you think somebody or something is funny or silly: The children laughed at the clown - They all laughed at me when I said I was afraid of dogs
the character who tells you what is happening in a book or film
very interesting: She told us fascinating stories about her life
If a place is teeming, it is full of people
any living thing that is not a plant: birds, fish and other creatures - This story is about creatures from another planet
not wearing any clothes (SAME MEANING: nude)

1. the state that somebody or something is in: The car was cheap and in good condition, so I bought it
2. conditions the situation in which people live, work or do things: The prisoners lived in terrible conditions
3. something that must happen before another thing can happen: One of the conditions of the job is that you agree to work on Saturdays
to start something new: The school was established in 1852
fictional fast food restaurant in the American animated television series SpongeBob SquarePants
fictional veggie burger sold by the fictional Krusty Krab restaurant
stories that somebody writes and that are not true: I enjoy reading fiction
vegetable
a plant or part of a plant that we eat: The students grow vegetables such as cabbages, beans and carrots

1. something that tells you that something exists, is happening or may heppen in the future: Dark clouds are a sign of rain
2. a thing with writing or a picture on it that tells you something: The sign said 'No Smoking' - a road sign
3. a mark, shape or movement that has a special meaning: In mathematics, a cross is a plus sign - I put up my hand as a sign for him to stop

1. to walk like a soldier: The soldiers marched along the road
2. to walk somewhere quickly in a determined way: She marched up to the manager and asked for her money back
3. to walk through the streets in a large group to show that you do not agree with something: They marched through the town shouting 'Stop the war!'

1. in a straight line: Look straight in front of you - Go straight on until you come to the bank, then turn left
2. without stopping or doing anything else; directly: Come straight home - She walked straight past me

1. to put somebody or something carefully on another thing: I laid the papers on the desk
2. to make an egg: birds and insects lay eggs

1. used to ask who something belongs to: Whose car is this?
2. used to say exactly which person or thing you mean, or to give extra information about a person or thing: That's the boy whose sister is a singer

1. to be somebody's: 'Who does this pen belong to?' 'It belongs to me'
2. to be a member of a group or an organization: Do you belong to any political party?
3. to have its right or usual place: That chair belongs in my room
a small, thin piece of wood or plastic used for removing food from between your teeth

1. a shape like a box with six square sides all the same size
2. the number that you get if you multiply a number by itself twice: The cube of 5 (= 5³) is 125 (= 5 * 5 * 5)

to move or do something quickly: We hurried home after school
**hurry up** to move or do something more quickly because there is not much time: Hurry up or we'll be late!
to get on a ship, plane, train, or other vehicle: We need to come aboard before the ship leaves

1. to buy or take something: Will you get some bread when you go shopping?
2. to receive something: I got a lot of presents for my birthday
3. to go and bring back somebody or something (SAME MEANING: fetch): Jenny will get the children from school
4. to become: He is getting fat - Mum got angry - It's getting cold
5. to arrive somewhere: We got to London at ten o'clock
6. a word that you use with part of another verb to show that something happens to somebody or something: She got caught by the police
7. to make somebody do something: I got Peter to help me
8. to start to have an illness: I think I'm getting a cold
9. to travel on something such as a train or a bus: I didn't walk _ I got the bus
10. to undersatand or hear something: I don't get the joke
**get away with something** to do something bad and not be punished for it: He lied but he got away with it
**get back** to return: When did you get back from your holiday?
**get in** to reach a place (SAME MEANING: arrive): My train got in at 7.15
**get in; get into something** to climb into a car: Tom got into the car
**get off; get off something** to leave something such as train, bus, or bycycle: Where did you get off the bus?
**get on** 1. words that you use to say or ask how well somebody does something: Patrick is getting on well at school - How did you get on in the exam?
2. to become old: My grandfather is getting on _ he's nearly 80
**get on; get onto something** to climb onto a bus, train, or bycycle: I got on the train
**get on with somebody** to live or work in a friendly way with somebody: We get on well with our neighbours
**get out; get out of something** to leave a car: I opened the door and got out
**get out of something** to not do something that you do not like: I'll come swimming with you if I can get out of cleaning my room
**get something out** to take something from the place where it was: She opened her bag and got out a pen
**get over something** to become well or happy again after you have been ill or sad: He still hasn't got over his wife's death
**get through** to be able to speak to somebody on the telephone: I tried to ring Kate but I couldn't get through
**get through something** to use or finish a certain amount of something: I got through a lot of work today
**get together** to meet; to come together in a group: The whole family got together for Christmas
**get up** 1. to stand up: He got up to let an elderly lady sit down
2. to get out of bed: What time do you usually get up?
**get up to something** 1. to do something, usually something bad: I must go and see what the children are getting up to
2. to reach a particular place, for example in a book: I've got up to page 180

1. to teach a person or an animal to do something: He was trained as a pilot
2. to make yourself ready for something by studying or doing something a lot: Ann is training to be a doctor - She's training for the Olympics
all the people who work on a ship or a plane
a boy or young man
to show that something is true: The blood on his shirt proves that he is the murderer (The noun is: proof)
to cook something in hot oil: I will fry the eggs for breakfast - Fry the onions in butter - fried eggs
to be able from your knowledge or experience to say that something is true
used by someone (such as a waiter) to say that something will be served or delivered very quickly: 'I'd like a turkey sandwich and a glass of lemonade, please.' 'Coming right up!'
a large person who often breaks things or has accidents
very bad: She had a terrible accident - The food in that restaurant is terrible!
a person who is traveling in a vehicle but not driving it: The plane was carrying 200 passengers
When baby birds, insects, or fish hatch, they come out of an egg

1. to have a particular smell: Dinner smells good - The perfume smells of roses
2. to notice something with your nose: Can you smell smoke?
3. to have a bad smell: Your feet smell
having a bad smell: smelly socks
a small or medium-sized oily sea fish
behaviour that is good, moral, and acceptable in society

1. with everything in the right place and done carefully (SAME MEANING: tidy): keep your room neat and tidy - She has very neat handwriting
2. (American, informal) good; nice: That's a really neat car

1. with everything in the right place: Her room is very tidy
2. liking to have everything in the right place: a tidy boy (OPPOSITE: untidy)
to fasten or secure something tightly, especially on a ship before a storm

1. to join or close something so that it will not come open: Please fasten your seat belts - Can you fasten this suitcase for me?
2. to fix or tie one thing to another thing: Fasten this badge to your jacket
over the side of a boat and into the water: She fell overboard

1. on somebody or something so that it covers them: She put a blanket over the sleeping child
2. above something; higher than something: A plane flew over our heads - There is a picture over the fireplace
3. across; to the other side of something: The dog jumped over the wall
4. to or in a place: Come over and see us on Saturday - Come over here! - Go over there and see if you can help
5. down or sideways: I fell over in the street - He learned over to speak to her
6. so that the other side is on top: You may turn your papers over and begin
7. more than a number, price, etc.: She lived in Mauritania for over 20 years - This game is for children of ten and over
8. not used; remaining: There are a lot of cakes left over from the party
9. used for saying that somebody repeats something: He said the same thing over and over again (= many times) - You'll have to start all over again (= from the beginning)
**all over** everywhere; in every part: Have you seen my glasses? I've looked all over - She travels all over the world

1. things that a shop keeps ready to sell: We have a large stock of tables and chairs - I'll see if we have your size in stock - I'm afraid that book's out of stock at the moment
2. (business) a share in a company or business that somebody has bought, or the value of those shares: stocks and shares
a girl or woman who is somebody's child: They have two daughters and a son - My oldest daughter is a doctor

1. beautiful or of good quality: There's a fine view from the cathedral - This is one of Monet's finest painting
2. well or happy: 'How are you?' 'Fine, thanks. And you?'
3. used for saying that something is good or acceptable (SAME MEANING: OK): 'Let's meet on Monday' 'Fine' - 'Do you want some more milk in your coffee?' 'No, that's fine'
4. not raining (SAME MEANING: sunny): I hope it stays fine for our picnic
5. very thin: I've got very fine hair (OPPOSITE: thick)
6. made of very small pieces: Salt is finer than sugar (OPPOSITE: coarse)
on or onto a ship, plane, train, or bus: Are all the passengers aboard the ship? - Welcome aboard flight 603 to Nairobi
a place to live, especially rooms or housing for soldiers, sailors, or servants
any valuable things or money stolen by an army at war or by thieves
a person who works for someone else for money: The company has 500 employees
the sharp pointed part of an insect or creature that can go into the skin leaving a smal, painful wound, sometimes with poison in it: a bee's stinger
a kind of shellfish which can be eaten
a type of sea creature with a shell, such as clams, oysters, mussels, shrimp, lobster, etc

1. the way that you do something or the way that something happens: Don't get angry. Let's try to talk about this in a calm manner
2. manners (plural) the way you behave when you are with other people: It's bad manners to talk with your mouth full
past simple and past participle of fight
to defeat someone
the hard protective outer covering of some animals such as a snail, mussel, crab, or turtle, or of a bird's egg
a word that we use to call a man
look at; to have a quick look: Let's take a gander at your new car
the hard outer covering of a tree
a strong, hard hat that covers and protects the head: a bicycle helmet - a war helmet
to move air or something else into and out of the lungs
to say something that it not true as a joke: I didn't mean it. I was only kidding - Are you kidding me?
not simple or ordinary; complicated or including a lot of features: I wanted a simple black dress, nothing fancy - a fancy restaurant
a visit to the most important countries and cities of Europe that rich young people made in the past as part of their education
a ball of gas that appears in a liquid, or a ball formed of air surrounded by liquid that floats in the air
to be or go all around something: Gwen sat at her desk, surrounded by books and papers

1. not mixed with anything else: pure orange juice
2. clean and healthy: pure mountain air
3. complete or total: What she said was pure nonsense
a building's roof that is rounded
a creature
a person who gives up easily instead of finishing something: I'm no quitter.

1. a word that you use with verbs like 'be', 'seem' and 'appear' to show that something is true or that something is happening: There is a man at the door - Is there a film on TV tonight? - There aren't any shops in this village
2. in, at or to that place: Don't put the box there _ put it here - Have you been to Khartoum? I'm going there next week
3. a word that makes people look or listen: Oh look, there's Kate
**there you are** words that you say when you give something to somebody: 'There you are,' she said, giving me a cake

1. When air or wind blows, it moves: The wind was from the sea
2. to move something through the air: The wind blew my hat off
3. to send air out from your mouth: He blew on the soup to cool it down - Please blow into this tube
4. to send air out from your mouth into a musical instrument, for example, to make a noise: The referee blew his whistle
**blow up; blow something up** 1. to explode or make something explode, for example with a bomb: The plane blew up - They blew up the bridge
2. to fill something with air: We blew up some balloons for the party
**blow your nose** to clear your nose by blowing strongly through it onto a piece of cloth or paper (called a handkerchief)
**blow it** 1. to fail at something: I really blew it in the exam
2. an expression of anger: Oh, blow it! I've forgotten to invite Paul to the party
(American, informal) (British mate) a friend: Hey buddy, how are you? - Don't worry buddy, You're doing fine

1. to try to get an answer by using a question: I asked him what the time was - 'What's your name?' she asked - Liz asked the teacher a question
2. to say that you would like somebody to do something for you: I asked Sara to drive me to the station
3. to try to get permission to do something: I asked my teacher if I could go home - I asked if I could go home early
4. to invite somebody to go somewhere with you: Mark has asked me to a party on Saturday
**ask for somebody** to say that you want to speak to somebody: Phone this number and ask for Mrs Green
**ask for something** to say that you want somebody to give you something: He asked for a new bike for his birthday
a sea creature that lives inside two joined flat, round shells and can be eaten
to make the short high sound that small birds make

1. with no fighting: a peaceful demonstration
2. quiet and calm: It's so peaceful here
making you feel calm: Her words had a soothing effect - I put on some nice soothing music

1. one of four equal parts of something; ¼: a mile and a quarter - The film starts in three quarters of an hour
2. three months: You get a telephone bill every quarter
3. a part of a town: the Chinese quarter

1. fair and ready to listen to what other people say: I tried to be reasonable even though I was very angry
2. fair or right in a particular situation: I think $100 is a reasonable price (OPPOSITE: unreasonable)

1. making a deep and loud hollow sound: a booming voice
2. increasing or becoming successful and producing a lot of money very quickly: Business is booming at the moment
to put something into a liquid for a short time: She dipped her toe into the pool to see how cold it was - Dip the fish in the batter, then drop it into the hot oil
with great attention: Drive carefully on those icy roads
if a person or thing is annoying, they make you a little angry: It's annoying when people don't listen to you
feeling sory and unhappy because you have done something wrong, or because you are not as good as other people: I was ashamed about lying to my parents - She was ashamed of her old clothes
only; not more than: She was a mere child when her parents died
to move your foot up and put it down in another place when you walk, run or dance: You stepped on my foot!
to turn around over and over very fast

a part of the DNA in a cell that controls the physical development, behaviour, etc. of an individual plant or animal and is passed on from its parents: The illness is believed to be caused by a defective gene
to pull apart; to tear, cut, or open or be torn something forcefully and quickly
smelling very bad
earth that has been mixed with water: The vehicles got bogged down in the heavy mud
a small pool of liquid on the ground, especially from rain
a person or animal that lives in a particular place: a city of five million inhabitants
to carefully watch something in order gain knowledge or understanding about the subject
If something is hilarious, it is extremely funny and makes you laugh a lot
very: They played extremely well - She's extremely beautiful
to make something move through the air by quickly moving your arm and hand

not including somebody or something: The restaurant is open every day except Sunday - Everyone went to the party except for me
**except that** apart from the fact that: I don't know what he looks like, except that he's very tall
someone who introduces programmes or reads the news on the television or radio
to move on sea waves by standing or lying on a special board

1. a distinctive characteristic or attribute
2. a name or symbol on a product that shows it was made by a particular company, and that it cannot be used by other companies without permission
Something that is distinctive is easy to recognize because it is different from other things
a feature or quality that somebody or something has
a thick and stiff type of paper material that is often used for packaging and making boxes

1. (informal) a man: He's a nice guy
2. guys (plural) (informal) used when speaking to a group of men and women or boys and girls: Come on guys, Let's go
a person whose job is making clothes, especially for men
a person who is watching an event, especially a sports event: There were 2000 spectators at the football match

words.mean_fa: بی‌جا، نابجا، نامناسب، غیرضروری (معمولاً برای رفتار یا حرفی که زننده، توهین‌آمیز یا بی‌ادبانه است)

words.mean_en: a round plastic disc used in some games that people can throw and catch in the air
to die under water because you cannot breathe; to make somebody die in this way: The boy fell in the river and drowned - Twenty people were drowned in the floods
a person who steals
to secretly take something that is not yours: Her money has been stolen
a person who behaves in a silly way without thinking: You fool, you missed your chance
a cream that you put on your skin to prevent it from being damaged by the sun
used to express pain: Ouch! That hurts!
to feel pain in a part of your body, or to injure someone or cause them pain: My head hurts
to hurt yourself or somebody else, especially in an accident: She injured her arm when she was playing tennis - Joe was injured in a car accident
a small round cake or piece of bread

1. to put a dead body in the ground
2. to put something in the ground or under something: The dog buried the bone in the garden
a person who wears funny clothes, has a painted face, and makes people laugh by performing tricks and behaving in a silly way
to feel sorry about something that you did or did not do: He regrets selling his car - I don't regret what I said to her
morally correct
